misconceptionSummary: Students compare decimal digits without aligning decimal points first.

steps.narration: Pad both decimals to the same number of decimal places. Now every digit lines up with its matching column — and comparison becomes straightforward.
Compare column by column from the left. The first column that differs settles it. You never need to look any further right.
The open end faces the bigger number — three point nine. Both statements say the same thing, just written in opposite directions.
Align, compare column by column, write the symbol. Three steps, every time.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: comparing 3.09 and 3.9 as if 09 > 9 (as integers). Always align decimal points and compare column by column.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Pad decimals to the same length before comparing. Without alignment, digit positions mean nothing.
